Purchasing Attributes The cost 0f an Attribute depends on its Attribute threshold, determined through the Life Path and your character'= age (see Age; 601. The threshold for each Attribute should be listed on your character creation worksheet: The cost in Character Points to raise an Attribute up to its threshold IS equal to the desired Attribute value_ Raising it above the threshold costs 2 addition- Character Points for every point above the threshold
Charisma (CHA) Charisma represents character presence charm and persuasiveness Related skills include those that involve inter- personae contact, such as Leadership and Negotiation. Players may be tempted to slight this Attribute in favor of more obvi: ously useful ones but high Charisma can open many doors and preventing combat through negotiation I5 better than duking out in many situations

Edge (EDG) Some people just seem t0 be born lucky: In MW3. those people have high Edge Attribute. Though related to only few skills_ this Attribute is valuable for all characters Unlike other Attributes, Edge can be used in game play t0 affect the roll of the dice (see Edge_ p. 18). little bit of luck can go long way;
Social Standing (SOC) The BattleTech universe iS dominated by feudal and caste driven societies_ and the idea of noble birth is widely accepted The Social Standing Attributc mcasurcs character' stalus among the socia strata 0f his chosen affiliation The specific definition of each Ievel of Social Standing varies by affiliation; generalizations are shown on the Attribute Table (p 541. high Social Standing Attribute denotes noble birth. but not necessarily noble title This important distinction is COV= ered in greater detail in the description f the Title Trait (p. 861.
Attributes also have minimums and maximums.
